text: Abstract: The global connectivity achieved by advances in information and communication technologies (ICTs) is affecting the way values and culture of various countries and societies are transformed. This research aims to examine the dynamics of globalization and local culture of Indonesia in the digital age. It explores the ways, either directly or indirectly, that values, traditions, beliefs and arts are influenced by the rapid development of ICTs and the increasing penetration of global civilization in which new media such as social media is becoming a dominant factor. This study is a qualitative descriptive study using literature review to investigate the mechanisms of cultural change and analyzing the impact of the emergence of culture 2.0 in Indonesia. The study found that ICTs bring about both opportunities and challenges at the global, national and local levels. These include opportunities such as improving cross-cultural understanding, creativity and online education, as well as challenges such as cultural imperialism, linguistic diversity loss, marginalization of ethnic cultural practices, and others. This study aims to analyze the variables affecting the culture of the Nation of Islands in the context of globalization and digital media, as well as economic globalization and urbanization. From this research, it can be concluded that in the digital age it is necessary to conduct a strategic adaptation in which the culture of Indonesia is promoted using digital media, and at the same time enhancing the role of nationalism in the country. In addition, it is also very important to conduct proactive preservation of local cultures so that they are not lost, through the implementation of culture education and international cooperation in order that Indonesia’s diverse cultures are preserved in the era of global connectivity.
